data.epi_content:
"Disgusting."

At least for now, she hasn't done anything to me. Hmm.

Actually, that's not true.

Perhaps this is the catalyst.

The reason I fell into this world is, in a way, somewhat forced but still because of Vivian.

Anyway, I'm trying to follow the timeline bit by bit.

From the very beginning, that time when I died pathetically, slitting my wrists and making a hole in my throat, crying and wailing.

I had an argument with Evan.

Today.

And today we didn't really fight much.

It doesn't matter.

It's enough to just get depressed and stay cooped up in this room all day.

Two days, three days, until all the food and chocolate in the room runs out and I start filling my stomach with water.

I won't go to school.

If I run into Lydia's damn face, I might just put a well-ventilated hole right between her eyebrows.

I suppose I'd be doing her a service by giving her one or two extra holes compared to others, but if I help her like that, I'll be in trouble too, so I can't.

To look outside the window, I got up, took out a cigar, and brought a cup of tea.

I went out to the veranda, leaned against the railing, looked down once, and then looked ahead again.

After lighting the cigar for about 3 minutes, smoke starts to rise.

I downed a cup of tea that had gone cold, then took a drag of smoke...

"Cough! Hack! Ugh."

Even while coughing violently, I kept it in my mouth and continued inhaling the smoke.

I'll get used to it gradually.

After about an hour, or after smoking one or two cigars.

Out of irritation, I threw the teacup I was holding outside.

There weren't many people passing by anyway.

With the sound of cutting through the wind, the teacup fell down and shattered into pieces on the ground with a loud noise.

Some people looked in the direction of the sound, but since no one was hurt, they just turned their heads once and continued on their way.

I didn't like that either.

By now, Father would have passed away, grieving in his room.

How did he die?

In the frame, I'm sitting with my hands folded neatly, and next to me, my brother is smiling broadly with his hand on my shoulder.

Father has a stern face, but with a slight smile at the corners of his lips, holding the hand of my birth mother—who surely must be loose, having six mistresses—with his left hand, while his right hand rests on the back of the chair I'm sitting on.

Six discovered at once—thinking about it, that was a bit much.

Father had at most two, no, three.

Displeased, I lit a fire at my fingertip and began heating the edge of the frame for quite some time.

The frame itself must have some special treatment because it wouldn't catch fire.

That's fine.

I had time to spare.

If I waited until Vivian came, that would be enough.

Strangely, while the frame wouldn't burn, the photo inside began to shrink due to the heat.

As the picture inside slowly changed color and began to shrink, flames started to appear from the frame with crackling sounds.

I took the heated frame and moved it to the veranda.

As the wind blew, small flames began to rise.

I got something like blisters on my hand while moving it, but it's just a little stinging, right?
data.epi_content2:
Nothing to worry about.

With the cigar in my mouth, I watched the portrait in the frame on the veranda burn away and unconsciously smiled.

It's ridiculous how similar it is to my own pathetic state.

Looking at the sight of a person burning to death, I laughed quietly at the irony—once I was sitting with my legs giving out, soiling myself while watching, and now I'm burning myself.

This is why being alone is comfortable.

When other humans are around, anxiety wells up, and when I do things that would make people point fingers and call me crazy, no one is here, so I feel at ease.

Why? It's enough just to not have to see a child playing with a ball in the park suddenly throw a stone and then crawl under a bench, trembling.

"By the way, what should I do when Vivian comes?"

Should I start thinking of Vivian as an enemy?

For that, she's already been imprinted in my mind and heart as a being far different from me, far superior to me...

Suddenly, a good idea came to mind.

I got up and walked to the entrance.

Then, I pulled the door open inward.

As expected, the door is made of wood.

I closed the door again, brought a chair from the room, and threw it at the door, creating a dent where it hit.

Then, if I shoot it, wouldn't it make a hole?

If one shot doesn't go through, I can just fire multiple shots.

With that thought, I rubbed the dented part with my hand, took out the pistol from my waist, pressed the muzzle against it, and pulled the trigger.

Bang!

The gunshot was louder than I expected.

My ears rang sharply, and my right wrist began to ache with a tingling sensation.

I guess I'm too weak to even handle the recoil properly.

The reason I hadn't felt pain until now was probably because the blood had rushed to my head.

Well, when a person gets angry or excited, they might not even notice a bullet grazing their head.

I shouldn't use such violent language.

It's not speech but thoughts.

Does it matter?

Well, people won't come rushing in.

They'll just glance at my door to see what happened.

After all, when I blew my throat out in the bathtub, not a single person came looking for me.

Was there anyone? I don't remember clearly because my consciousness was hazy at the end.

Anyway, there was definitely a round hole in the door now.

Big enough for a pinky finger to fit through.

I took a piece of cloth and plugged the hole, then moved a chair in front of the door, sat down with my legs crossed, and smoked a cigar.

When hungry, I eat chocolate; when thirsty, I brew and drink tea.

When my body itches, I take a shower, and when I get too bored, I just play an interesting game with only one bullet loaded.

To keep my promise not to kill myself, the muzzle was pointed at my foot, not my temple.

It looked quite ridiculous.

Truly, half-wishing to die.

But unlike before when I lacked the courage to die and would put only one or two bullets in, press the muzzle to my temple, and pull the trigger, I didn't risk my life this time. Perhaps the goddess of fortune was angry at this, so she made a small hole in my foot.

Thanks to that, I let out a low moan, though not quite a scream, dug out the bullet with my finger, and wrapped it with a white cloth.

It stings a little when I walk.

Evan came looking for me several times after that day, but I sent him away each time.

The wound on my foot could be perfectly hidden just by wearing a sock, so it wasn't discovered.

How many days have passed?

The cigars I was smoking ran out long ago, so I'm enjoying smoking by putting paper and sawdust in a pipe with some tobacco cut into it.

And today, finally, the container that was full of chocolate has started to show its bottom.
data.epi_content3:
Unless I go to a store and buy something to eat, I'll have to starve, but I don't want to go out again.

"When will Vivian come looking for me?"

I stood in front of the door for a day or two, continuously waiting for someone.

Knock knock.

After lunch, when I was feeling a bit hungry and had picked up five or six chocolates to eat, then was passing time smoking tobacco, a knocking sound came.

I had stayed up all night waiting, so my head was foggy.

My throat was so dry it felt like it was about to burn up, making it difficult to speak properly, and when I stepped forward, a thudding sound was heard.

When there was no answer, the knocking sound came again.

First, I drank in one gulp a cup of green tea from the table that had gone cold and would surely taste bitter, then took out the pistol from my waist.

"Miss, I came because I was worried. Could you please open the door..."

She says she's worried.

Well, shouldn't you worry about yourself first?

The voice sounds like a woman's, so at least it's not Evan.

It wouldn't matter if it was Evan.

Whether a bullet hits him or not, he can heal himself.

Did Vivian first come looking for me like this?

I can't remember well.

Oh, I don't know.

I pressed the muzzle against the door and gripped my right hand tightly with my left to stop it from shaking.

Then I pulled the trigger, and the sound echoed through the room.

Bang.

"Eh."

When I opened the door, what I saw wasn't Vivian or Evan, but Lydia clutching her stomach and staggering, along with two unknown girls.

Looking at their expressions, they seemed quite shocked.

"Ah, I made a mistake."

I took the cigar from my mouth with my left hand, feeling the air fill with smelly smoke, and spoke quietly to Lydia.

"Lydia, it's been a while."

Looking at her stomach, the wound isn't that big.

Blood isn't spreading like paint on white paper.

It just seems to have grazed her skin.

Maybe because it went through the door first, the power was too weakened.

"Please step aside."

I pointed the gun at the two children standing next to Lydia.

They immediately started running away.

"M-Miss...?"

Lydia just looked at me with bewildered eyes, too shocked to do anything else.

Not wanting to bother blowing her head off, I struck her philtrum with the handle.

"So, Lydia. Why did you come looking for me?"

When I asked Lydia while pointing the gun at her, she couldn't answer and just trembled.

It was just ridiculous.

I was ridiculous too.

Just, like that.
